a: GTA VI is currently targeting November 19, 2026. Release windows can shift, so this countdown uses the latest configured date and updates instantly if it changes.

q: Will GTA VI launch on PC?
a: The confirmed launch platforms are PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S. A PC version is widely expected to follow after the console release, as has been the pattern with previous Rockstar titles.
GTA VI returns to Vice City — Rockstar's stylized vision of Miami — within the broader state of Leonida, blending dense neon cityscapes with beaches, swamps and rural backroads.
The game stars two playable protagonists, Lucia and Jason. Lucia is the first female lead in a mainline Grand Theft Auto game, and their intertwined story drives the campaign.
Preload details have not been finalized. Typically, digital preloads open a few days before launch on PlayStation and Xbox storefronts. Keep an eye on official Rockstar channels closer to release.

q: Is there an online mode at launch?
a: The initial focus is the single-player experience. Online multiplayer is planned to arrive after launch as a separate, evolving mode rather than on day one.

q: How much will GTA VI cost?
a: Official pricing has not been confirmed. Expect standard and premium editions in line with other major current-gen releases. This site will not sell or take pre-orders — it's a fan countdown.

q: Is this an official Rockstar website?
a: No. This is an independent, non-commercial fan project built to count down to launch. All trademarks belong to their respective owners. For official news, visit Rockstar Games directly.
